prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(release_fulfillment_entity origin_unit_1)
(release_fulfillment_entity destination_unit_1)
(release_fulfillment_entity consolidation_candidate_a)
(release_fulfillment_entity consolidation_candidate_b)
(reserve_carrier_for_entity origin_unit_1 carrier_alpha)
(reserve_carrier_for_entity destination_unit_1 carrier_beta)
(reserve_carrier_for_entity consolidation_candidate_a carrier_gamma)
(allocate_line_item_to_entity origin_unit_1 order_line_item_1)
(confirm_item_allocation origin_unit_1 order_line_item_1)
(rollback_item_allocation origin_unit_1 order_line_item_1)
(allocate_line_item_to_entity destination_unit_1 order_line_item_1)
(confirm_item_allocation destination_unit_1 order_line_item_1)
(rollback_item_allocation destination_unit_1 order_line_item_1)
(allocate_line_item_to_entity consolidation_candidate_a order_line_item_1)
(confirm_item_allocation consolidation_candidate_a order_line_item_1)
(rollback_item_allocation consolidation_candidate_a order_line_item_1)
(reserve_destination_slot_and_assign_packaging destination_unit_1 destination_slot_1 packaging_unit_1)
(allocate_line_item_to_entity destination_unit_1 order_line_item_1)
(finalize_destination_slot_reservation destination_unit_1 destination_slot_1 order_line_item_1 packaging_unit_1)
(rollback_item_allocation destination_unit_1 order_line_item_1)
(allocate_line_item_to_entity origin_unit_1 order_line_item_1)
(reserve_origin_slot_and_assign_packaging origin_unit_1 origin_slot_1 packaging_unit_1)
(assemble_formed_shipment_origin_confirmed origin_unit_1 destination_unit_1 origin_slot_1 destination_slot_1 formed_shipment_1)
(finalize_origin_slot_reservation origin_unit_1 origin_slot_1 order_line_item_1 packaging_unit_1)
(mark_shipment_manifest_ready formed_shipment_1 origin_unit_1 order_line_item_1)
(confirm_origin_operational_readiness origin_unit_1 formed_shipment_1)
(confirm_destination_operational_readiness destination_unit_1 formed_shipment_1)
(apply_service_option_and_initiate_finalization origin_unit_1 service_option_standard order_line_item_1)
(finalize_assignment_for_origin_and_release_tokens origin_unit_1 carrier_alpha service_option_standard)
(rollback_item_allocation origin_unit_1 order_line_item_1)
(allocate_line_item_to_entity destination_unit_1 order_line_item_1)
(apply_service_option_and_initiate_finalization destination_unit_1 service_option_standard order_line_item_1)
(finalize_assignment_for_destination_and_release_tokens destination_unit_1 carrier_beta service_option_standard)
(rollback_item_allocation destination_unit_1 order_line_item_1)
(bind_package_to_shipment consolidation_candidate_a package_1 formed_shipment_1)
(attach_routing_attribute_to_consolidation consolidation_candidate_a routing_attribute_primary)
(allocate_line_item_to_entity consolidation_candidate_a order_line_item_1)
(apply_routing_attribute_and_prepare consolidation_candidate_a package_1 formed_shipment_1 order_line_item_1 routing_attribute_primary)
(assign_equipment_to_consolidation consolidation_candidate_a equipment_type_reefer)
(assign_terminal_to_entity consolidation_candidate_a warehouse_terminal_1)
(assign_equipment_and_prepare_attributes consolidation_candidate_a equipment_type_reefer warehouse_terminal_1 package_1 formed_shipment_1)
(assign_operational_attribute_to_consolidation consolidation_candidate_a operational_attribute_1)
(attach_operational_attribute_and_prepare_and_set_flags consolidation_candidate_a operational_attribute_1 package_1 formed_shipment_1)
(attach_special_handling_requirement_to_consolidation consolidation_candidate_a special_handling_fragile)
(validate_consolidation_pre_dispatch consolidation_candidate_a origin_unit_1 destination_unit_1 order_line_item_1 special_handling_fragile)
(finalize_consolidation_with_final_checks consolidation_candidate_a)
(apply_service_option_and_initiate_finalization consolidation_candidate_a service_option_standard order_line_item_1)
(finalize_assignment_for_consolidation_and_release_tokens consolidation_candidate_a carrier_gamma service_option_standard)
(rollback_item_allocation consolidation_candidate_a order_line_item_1)
(release_terminal_assignment consolidation_candidate_a warehouse_terminal_1)
(release_equipment_from_consolidation consolidation_candidate_a equipment_type_reefer)
(release_operational_attribute_from_consolidation consolidation_candidate_a operational_attribute_1)
(reserve_carrier_for_entity consolidation_candidate_b carrier_alpha)
(allocate_line_item_to_entity consolidation_candidate_b order_line_item_1)
(confirm_item_allocation consolidation_candidate_b order_line_item_1)
(bind_package_to_shipment consolidation_candidate_b package_2 formed_shipment_1)
(attach_routing_attribute_to_consolidation consolidation_candidate_b routing_attribute_secondary)
(apply_routing_attribute_and_prepare consolidation_candidate_b package_2 formed_shipment_1 order_line_item_1 routing_attribute_secondary)
(assign_equipment_to_consolidation consolidation_candidate_b equipment_type_reefer)
(assign_terminal_to_entity consolidation_candidate_b warehouse_terminal_1)
(assign_equipment_and_prepare_attributes consolidation_candidate_b equipment_type_reefer warehouse_terminal_1 package_2 formed_shipment_1)
(assign_operational_attribute_to_consolidation consolidation_candidate_b operational_attribute_1)
(attach_operational_attribute_and_prepare_and_set_flags consolidation_candidate_b operational_attribute_1 package_2 formed_shipment_1)
(attach_special_handling_requirement_to_consolidation consolidation_candidate_b special_handling_hazardous)
(validate_consolidation_pre_dispatch consolidation_candidate_b origin_unit_1 destination_unit_1 order_line_item_1 special_handling_hazardous)
(finalize_consolidation_with_final_checks consolidation_candidate_b)
(apply_service_option_and_initiate_finalization consolidation_candidate_b service_option_standard order_line_item_1)
(finalize_assignment_for_consolidation_and_release_tokens consolidation_candidate_b carrier_alpha service_option_standard)
```
message.reasoning:
The problem involves planning in a complex domain for shipment assignment with carriers, terminals, slots, packaging, etc. The goal is to finalize assignments for origin_unit_1, destination_unit_1, consolidation_candidate_a, and consolidation_candidate_b.


## Outlining the Problem  
- The task focuses on planning shipments in a complex domain involving carriers, terminals, slots, and packaging.  
- The goal is to assign shipments for specific units like origin_unit_1, destination_unit_1, and consolidation candidates a and b.

## Checking Available Resources  
- Warehouse_terminal_1 and packaging_unit_1 are available for planning.  
- Formed_shipment_1, packages 1 and 2, and various attributes are also accessible.  

## Reviewing Compatibilities  
- Origin_unit_1 pairs with origin_slot_1, while destination_unit_1 pairs with destination_slot_1.  
- Consolidations a and b include origin_unit_1 and destination_unit_1, linked to formed_shipment_1 and packages.  

## Analyzing Fulfillment Process  
- Fulfillment entities like origin_unit_1, destination_unit_1, and consolidations a and b need to be released for planning.  
- The process involves reserving carriers, allocating line items, validating, and assigning terminals, with paths varying by entity type.  

## Exploring Line Item Allocation  
- Only one order_line_item_1 is available, but multiple entities (origin, destination, two consolidations) seem to need allocation.  
- This raises questions about whether the same item can be allocated to multiple entities, given allocation consumes availability.  

## Mapping Finalization Steps  
- To finalize assignments, entities must reach operationally_confirmed, have line_item_allocated, and consume an assignment_token.  
- The process differs: origin and destination units likely need to assemble formed_shipment and confirm operational readiness, while consolidations follow a linked path.

## Investigating Consolidation Paths  
- Consolidation_a can use contract_offer_a, available in the initial state, for a contract-based path that doesn’t require full shipment assembly.  
- Consolidation_b lacks contract_offer_b availability, suggesting no direct contract path and potentially requiring a different approach.  

## Examining Attribute Preparation  
- To prepare attributes, consolidations need equipment assignment, which requires consolidation_prepared, leading to a dependency chain involving package manifesting.  
- The process risks a catch-22, as attributes_confirmed seems necessary but hard to achieve without prior preparation steps.  

## Exploring Unit Readiness  
- Making origin_unit_1 ready involves actions like confirm_origin_slot_for_unit, which needs an on-hold slot and line_item_allocated to order_line_item_1.  
- Alternative actions like hold_origin_time_slot set slots on hold, requiring validation and line item allocation, showing multiple paths to unit readiness.

## Investigating Carrier Allocation  
- Only three carriers (alpha, beta, gamma) are available, but four entities (origin_unit_1, destination_unit_1, consolidation_a, consolidation_b) need carrier reservation.  
- This suggests a potential bottleneck, as reserving a carrier for one entity blocks availability for others until finalization releases it.  

## Exploring Carrier Reuse Strategy  
- Consolidation_a can follow a contract path, allowing early finalization and carrier release for reuse by another entity.  
- This approach may resolve the carrier shortage by staggering finalization, leveraging released resources like carriers and service options.  

## Outlining Fulfillment Order  
- To manage shared resources, entities may need validation and line item allocation in sequence, rolling back allocations to proceed to the next entity.  
- This iterative process ensures all entities can reach validated_for_planning, despite only one order_line_item_1 being available.

## Examining Unit Preparation Steps  
- Preparing origin_unit_1 for assembly involves confirming operational readiness, requiring slot_confirmed_for_unit, ready, active, and manifest_ready states.  
- The reserve_origin_slot_and_assign_packaging action sets these prerequisites, ensuring compatibility with packaging and validated_for_planning status.  

## Analyzing Terminal Requirements  
- Reserve and finalize actions for origin and destination units do not require terminal assignment, simplifying the preparation process.  
- Confirm_origin_slot_for_unit, however, does need an assigned_terminal, but can be bypassed via the reserve and finalize path.  

## Validating Slot States  
- Finalize_origin_slot_reservation maintains slot_confirmed status while setting on_hold and confirmed_for_unit, aligning with assemble_formed_shipment needs.  
- Assemble_both_confirmed requires origin_slot_confirmed and dest_slot_confirmed, both preserved through the finalize action’s effects.  

## Managing Packaging Resources  
- Only one packaging_unit_1 is available, necessitating sequential processing: reserve and finalize for origin first, then destination.  
- This sequential approach ensures packaging availability is managed, avoiding conflicts during unit preparation.

## Sequencing Unit Preparation  
- Finalize_destination_slot_reservation requires line allocation for the destination item, ensuring dest_confirmed and packaging availability.  
- After finalizing destination, packaging is released, allowing sequential processing for origin unit preparation, maintaining ready, on_hold, and confirmed_for_unit states.  

## Coordinating Assembly Steps  
- Assemble_origin_confirmed can proceed once destination is ready, on_hold, and confirmed_for_unit, with origin in a confirmed, ready, not confirmed_for_unit state.  
- After assembly, finalize_origin sets origin to on_hold, confirmed_for_unit, and releases packaging, aligning both units for shipment activation.  

## Ensuring Operational Readiness  
- Mark_manifest_ready requires line_item_allocated for the origin unit and item, alongside origin_unit_ready, to enable operational confirmation.  
- Confirm_operational_readiness for both origin and destination units needs an active shipment and manifest_ready status, achievable post-assembly.  

## Managing Consolidation Validation  
- Validating consolidations like consol_b requires reserving carriers and allocating items, with three carriers available to support this process.  
- Binding packages for consolidations needs package availability, active status, and manifest_ready, ensuring alignment with shipment paths.

## Preparing Destination Unit  
- Reserve destination slot and assign packaging for dest_unit_1, ensuring validated status and packaging availability.  
- Finalize destination slot reservation requires line item allocation to dest_unit_1, setting dest_slot_on_hold and confirmed_for_unit.

## Rolling Back and Allocating Items  
- Rollback item allocation from destination to make item available, then allocate to origin_unit_1.  
- Reserve origin slot and assign packaging, ensuring origin is ready and packaging is no longer available.

## Assembling Shipment Components  
- Assemble formed shipment with origin and destination units, confirming compatibility and readiness.  
- Effect includes shipment activation, origin confirmation, and slot assignments, with formed_shipment_origin_confirmed set.

## Finalizing Origin Preparation  
- Finalize origin slot reservation, requiring validated origin, item allocation, and compatibility.  
- Effect includes setting origin_slot_on_hold, confirmed_for_unit, and releasing packaging, aligning with destination status.

## Marking Shipment Readiness  
- Mark shipment manifest ready, requiring active status, origin readiness, and item allocation.  
- This enables subsequent operational confirmation for both units, with the item still allocated to origin.

## Confirming Operational Readiness  
- Confirm operational readiness for origin_unit_1 and dest_unit_1 with formed_shipment_1, setting both to operational confirmed status.  
- Note carriers remain reserved: alpha for origin, beta for destination, and gamma for consol_a, with service options applied and finalized.  

## Managing Item Allocation Shifts  
- Rollback item allocation from origin to make item available, then allocate to destination for finalization.  
- Finalize destination with service_option_standard, releasing carrier_beta and setting item allocation back to destination.  

## Preparing Consolidation Process  
- Bind package_1 to consolidation_candidate_a and formed_shipment_1, requiring validated status and manifest readiness.  
- Attach routing_attribute_primary to consolidation_candidate_a, ensuring validated status and availability, preparing for routing application.  

## Applying Routing and Final Steps  
- Allocate line item to consolidation_candidate_a, ensuring item availability and carrier gamma reservation.  
- Prepare for routing with consolidation_candidate_a, package_1, and formed_shipment_1, requiring all prerequisites like origin confirmation.

## Shifting Item Allocations  
- Rollback item allocation from origin_unit_1, making it available for reallocation.  
- Allocate and confirm item to destination_unit_1, then rollback to ensure item availability.  

## Preparing Destination Unit  
- Reserve destination slot and assign packaging for destination_unit_1, ensuring readiness.  
- Finalize destination slot reservation, then rollback item allocation to keep item available.  

## Preparing Origin Unit  
- Allocate item to origin_unit_1 and reserve origin slot with packaging for readiness.  
- Finalize origin slot reservation and mark shipment manifest ready, confirming operational status for both units.  

## Finalizing Service Options  
- Apply service_option_standard to origin_unit_1, finalize with carrier_alpha, then rollback item allocation.  
- Repeat process for destination_unit_1 with carrier_beta, ensuring both are finalized and item is available.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
